issues.description: `animId` is declared as `let animId: number` (implicitly `undefined`) but the cleanup function at line 181 calls `cancelAnimationFrame(animId)` unconditionally. If the component unmounts before `document.fonts.ready` resolves (line 162), `animId` was never assigned, so `cancelAnimationFrame(undefined)` is called. While this is a no-op in browsers today, it is technically undefined behavior per the spec and could produce a runtime warning in strict environments.
issues.fix: Initialize `animId` to 0: `let animId: number = 0;`. `cancelAnimationFrame(0)` is a valid no-op.

issues.description: `handleSignIn` is an async function that calls `signInWithProvider` without try/catch. If `signInWithProvider` throws (e.g., network error, browser popup blocked), the error is silently swallowed because the onClick handler returns a floating promise. Users will see no feedback and the button will appear to do nothing.
issues.fix: Wrap in try/catch or handle the error (show toast/error state): `try { await signInWithProvider(...); } catch (err) { /* handle error */ }`

issues.description: `handleCopyUserId` is an async function that calls `navigator.clipboard.writeText` without try/catch. The Clipboard API throws `DOMException: NotAllowedError` in non-HTTPS contexts or when clipboard permission is denied. The error will be an unhandled promise rejection from the onClick handler.
issues.fix: Add try/catch around the clipboard call and optionally show a fallback or toast on error.

issues.description: `handleMoveUp` and `handleMoveDown` issue two sequential `updateMutation.mutate()` calls to swap `sort_order` values between two items. Both mutations fire immediately with the current `sort_order` values. If either mutation fails partway through, the two items will have the same `sort_order` value (partial swap). There is no rollback or optimistic-update pattern here. This is not a crash bug but can result in inconsistent ordering state persisted to the server.
issues.fix: Consider using a single server action or sequential mutation (chain onSuccess) to atomically swap the two sort_order values, or implement optimistic-update with rollback.

patterns.pattern: Floating promises from async onClick handlers
patterns.description: Several components use `onClick={() => asyncFn()}` which creates floating promises. Errors in these handlers are silently ignored. The affected files are SocialLoginButtons.tsx and AuditRowDetail.tsx. Pattern is not widespread but worth noting for future handlers.
patterns.suggestion: Consider a wrapper utility like `void asyncFn()` with a .catch, or ensure async handlers always have try/catch.

patterns.description: All setInterval and setTimeout usages inspected (FeaturedRibbon, ReceivedOrderCard, ExpirationLabel, useAutoSavePaymentMethod, SearchBar, ProductFilters, UsersPageContent, useAddToCart) have proper cleanup via return functions in useEffect. The cartCookie subscribeToCartCookie correctly returns a cleanup function that removes all addEventListener registrations.
